highlights.text: The cycle goes as follows: ES goes elevator down.
Then, ES puts in a Failed Breakdown.
An elevator down sell to setup a Failed Breakdown.
This was an “elevator down” sell, meaning we prepare for the Failed Breakdown and short-squeeze.

The long setup for this was the Failed Breakdown of a big low at 6605 - given in advance to readers - and we squeezed 158 points yesterday to hit 6766 *exact* to set the high of day yesterday, from which we sold 112 points yesterday.
You can see here the cycle in play: Elevator down sell, Failed Breakdown, short squeeze to back-test.
After yesterday’s 112 point flush to 6652, we got another Failed Breakdown.
If not, wait for the Failed Breakdown of that 6667 low.” We hit 6652 low of day yesterday, recovered 6667, then ripped to 6752 by this morning.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the 158 point Failed Breakdown we had from the 6605 Failed Breakdown yesterday to the 6766 macro back-test target Wednesday.
This was the 2nd best Failed Breakdown of 2025, so its important to understand it.
I specified this target at the close on Monday, stating: “Targets are 6715-23 which tackles the red “megaphone” above yet again.
This back-test remains a magnet.” ES we would need a setup to rally us there.

post_title: SPX Back-Tested Last Friday's Breakdown Yesterday And Collapsed Today. Are Bulls In Trouble? Oct 17 Plan
